_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Globe: Game 28 — Red Sox at Orioles preview and lineups as Boston proceeds without Cora
items.snark: First lineup of the post-Cora era and Tracy's just trying to remember which Mayer is which. Globe will run the same preview tomorrow with two adjective swaps.

items.headline: Globe: Red Sox fire manager Alex Cora after abysmal start to 2026 season
items.snark: Eight years and a ring, gone two hours after the lineup hung 17. Henry's timing as bad as Breslow's roster math.

items.headline: Globe: Why now, what's next — major questions looming after the Cora firing
items.snark: Globe lists the questions, Globe owns the team that has to answer them. Conflict of interest writes itself.

items.headline: Globe (Shaughnessy): It wasn't Cora's fault the roster stinks, and he shouldn't have been fired over it
items.snark: CHB cranking out the same column he wrote in '11, '12, and '20 — the bit still works because the ownership group still doesn't.

items.headline: Over the Monster: Cora wasn't to blame for the Sox' slow start, but maybe it was time to move on
items.snark: OTM lands the Shaughnessy haymaker then folds in a 'but maybe though.' SB Nation house style: have the take, refund half of it.

items.headline: SI: Red Sox' season was doomed from the start, and firing Cora won't change that
items.snark: Payroll built like a Spirit flight to Buffalo, nobody up front can manage their way out of that. Stewardess didn't crash the plane.

items.headline: SI: Cora's firing is the final straw — John Henry should sell the Red Sox
items.snark: Even SI is on the 'Sell the team' chant now. Henry's PR moat shrinking faster than the LIV-money guys' welcome at the Country Club.

items.headline: Yahoo Sports: Cora had rare managerial power, yet the Red Sox fired the untouchable
items.snark: Untouchable? Made the playoffs in 2024, axed by April 2026. Yawkey Way's 'untouchable' has the shelf life of a Pats coach not named Belichick.

items.headline: FanSided: The Red Sox Wedding — Breslow didn't just fire Cora, he cleaned house
items.snark: Breslow ran the Red Wedding and somehow he's the one left in the dining hall sipping wine. Game of Thrones except every Stark works in advanced analytics.

items.headline: Boston.com: 5 things to know about Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy
items.snark: Forty years old, four years at Pawtucket, fluent in twenty-something. Bres found his guy at Worcester for the price of a bus ticket.

items.headline: MLBTR: Red Sox select RHP Jack Anderson, designate Tyler Samaniego
items.snark: Bullpen so cooked they're calling up Anderson off the 40-man — kid's gonna get the ball in the seventh down four and have to act surprised.

items.headline: Boston.com: Red Sox erupt for 10 runs in the ninth, beat Orioles 17-1
items.snark: Twenty-seven games to figure out hitting, then ten runs in one inning against a Camden bullpen that looked like it qualified through Pop Warner.

items.headline: Over the Monster: Red Sox 17, Orioles 1 — Crochet and offense snap skid
items.snark: Crochet found his strike zone (six shutout, seven K) the same day the front office found its courage. Coincidence, definitely.

items.headline: FanGraphs: Crochet's struggles are just the start of Boston's problems
items.snark: FanGraphs ran the regression and somehow the curve points downward. Might want to update the resume, Bres.

items.headline: Pats Pulpit: Quick-hit thoughts on the Patriots' 2026 NFL Draft class
items.snark: Pulpit's quick hits: Lomu nasty, Jacas raw, Wolf maybe-OK. The 'maybe-OK' bit doing more lifting than the o-line did last year.

items.headline: Pats Pulpit: Full 2026 draft class grades — Lomu, Jacas, Raridon headline
items.snark: Lomu, Jacas, Raridon get the love; the Day 3 kids get a participation B-minus. Wolf grading himself with a calculator he bought.

items.headline: Pats Pulpit: UDFA tracker — every undrafted rookie New England signed
items.snark: Harvard lineman with a $5K signing bonus — dude could've made that buying back his Crimson Tide stock. Welcome to Foxborough.

items.headline: PatsFans: Patriots News 04-26 — Meet the 2026 Draft Class
items.snark: Daily roundup says the rookie class is locked in. Meanwhile the head coach is locked in counseling and the GM is locked in his office stress-eating granola.

items.headline: Yahoo: Vrabel declines to commit to Patriots activities post-draft as Russini deletes X account
items.snark: Head coach won't say if he's at OTAs, the reporter nuked her account, and the Krafts pretend the rookie minicamp is the only news that matters.

items.headline: ESPN: Mike Vrabel to seek counseling, won't be with Patriots for NFL Draft Day 3
items.snark: Wolf solo on the headset because the head coach is away. Front office covered it in two PR releases; Page Six covered it in fourteen photos.

items.headline: NBCS: NFL Draft Day 3 recap — Patriots bolster depth, take flyer on QB
items.snark: Wolf grabbed depth, a backup QB, and a kid named Quintayvious. That last name's gonna get butchered on every PA system from Foxborough to Buffalo.

items.headline: Pats Pulpit: Full 2026 rookie class tracker, all six Day 3 picks
items.snark: Eight new helmets in the building, the head coach watching from a wellness retreat. Only the Pats.

items.headline: CBS Sports: Patriots remain frontrunners for A.J. Brown post-draft
items.snark: Whole trade hinges on a June 1 cap-hit calendar. Wolf's running fantasy football at his actual day job.

items.headline: Heavy: Caleb Lomu shares excitement about blocking for Drake Maye
items.snark: Rookie tackle says he's pumped to protect Maye — great, just don't watch the 2025 game film of who used to play right tackle.

items.headline: Patriots.com: Experts react to Patriots' 2026 NFL Draft Day 3 picks
items.snark: The Krafts pay the in-house staff to type 'high upside' on every pick and they earned the bonus today.

items.headline: Globe: NFL Draft 2026 winners and losers from Night 2
items.snark: Globe gave the Pats a B-plus for Day 2, the same grade Volin gives every Pats pick since the Belichick era ended.

items.headline: NBCS: Patriots add Gabe Jacas, Eli Raridon on Day 2 after Round 2 trade-up
items.snark: Edge rusher and a 6-foot-6 tight end from Notre Dame. Wolf's building the world's most aggressive pickup-truck commercial.
